features.summary: Facebook reported strong Q2 2021 results with total revenue of $29.1 billion, up 56% year-over-year, driven primarily by advertising revenue of $28.6 billion. Daily active users reached 1.91 billion, up 7% compared to last year, with approximately 3.5 billion people using at least one Facebook service monthly. The company added over 2,700 net new hires in Q2, ending with over 63,400 full-time employees, up 21% year-over-year.
features.management_outlook: Management expects advertising revenue growth to be primarily driven by price increases rather than impression growth for the remainder of 2021, with year-over-year total revenue growth rates expected to decelerate significantly in Q3 and Q4 as they lap periods of strong growth. They anticipate increasing ad targeting headwinds from iOS updates to have a more significant impact in Q3 compared to Q2. Total expenses are expected to be in the range of $70-73 billion for 2021, with capital expenditures of $19-21 billion, reflecting continued investment in technical talent, infrastructure, and consumer hardware.
features.risks: The company faces increasing ad targeting headwinds from regulatory and platform changes, particularly iOS 14.5 updates which are expected to have greater impact in Q3. Management continues to monitor developments regarding transatlantic data transfers and their potential impact on European operations. The shift toward video content and new surfaces like Reels currently monetizes at lower rates than traditional feed and stories, creating near-term revenue pressure despite long-term opportunities.
features.key_drivers: Revenue growth was driven by strong advertiser demand across verticals, with e-commerce, retail, and CPG performing well, plus recovery in travel and entertainment sectors. The company is investing heavily in three strategic areas: creator economy (with $1 billion commitment), commerce infrastructure to create native shopping experiences, and building the metaverse as the next computing platform. Video content, particularly short-form video like Reels, is becoming increasingly important, now accounting for almost half of time spent on Facebook and driving engagement growth on Instagram.
